info.title: Dota 2 Auction Draft API
info.description: REST API for the Dota 2 Auction Draft platform. Manages competitions, players, captains, auctions, tournaments, and user accounts.

tags.name: Fantasy
tags.description: Fantasy league stages, picks, and leaderboard

tags.name: Discord
tags.description: Discord bot settings — admin panel for the discord/ service

tags.name: Seasons
tags.description: Ranked seasons — ELO ladders attached to queue pools

tags.name: Home
tags.description: Aggregated data for the public home page (stats, featured tournament, top players, hero pick rate)

tags.name: MMR Verifications
tags.description: User-submitted MMR change requests with screenshot proof; admins approve to update players.mmr

tags.name: Admin Stats
tags.description: Request and socket-event observability for admins

tags.name: Search
tags.description: Global search across players and matches

tags.name: Subscription Plans
tags.description: Tiered membership plans + manual user assignments. Pricing/perks placeholders today; future payment-provider webhooks will populate user_subscriptions with source != 'manual'.

online.description: Present only on /api/friends; true when the other player has any active socket.

blindBidTimer.description: Seconds for blind bid phase (separate from open bid timer)

autoFinish.description: Auto-finish draft when all teams are full. If false, admin must end manually.

xpMatchWin.description: XP for winning a match series

xpFantasyParticipation.description: XP for participating in a fantasy stage

xpFantasyStageWinner.description: XP for winning a fantasy stage

rules_title.description: Heading shown above the rules block on /queue when this pool is selected. Empty = falls back to the localized 'Rules' label.

rules_content.description: Sanitized HTML body shown in the collapsible rules card on /queue. Empty = no rules card is rendered for this pool.

queue_count.description: Number of players currently waiting in this pool. Included on the public /api/queue/pools listing for pool-selector badges; not persisted in the DB.

captain_eligibility_threshold.description: MMR window for random captain selection. Players within this many MMR of the lobby's top get a random chance to be captain. Set to 0 to always pick the top 2 by MMR (legacy behavior).

rules_title.description: Heading for the rules block. Optional.

rules_content.description: Sanitized HTML body for the rules block. Optional.

get.description: Returns the deploy-time version token from server/.version. Clients poll this every 30s to detect new deploys and prompt users to refresh.

Returns 200 with `status: ok` when the process is up and the database is reachable (verified via `SELECT 1`). Returns 503 with `status: error` if the DB query fails. Suitable for readiness probes and external uptime monitors. For pure liveness (process-up only, no DB), use `/api/live` instead.

Returns 200 with `status: ok` whenever the process is responsive. Does NOT touch the database, so a transient DB outage will not flip this endpoint to unhealthy. Use this for container liveness probes; use `/api/health` for readiness/uptime monitoring.

get.summary: Get the status transition timeline for a bot
get.description: Requires the `manage_bots` permission. Returns the most recent status changes (newest first, up to 100) recorded for the bot. Every status transition on `lobby_bots` is captured by a DB trigger, so the timeline reflects changes from any source — manual connect/disconnect, auto-connect, lobby lifecycle, or the Go bot service.

post.summary: Connect every offline or errored bot
post.description: Requires the `manage_bots` permission. Iterates all bots whose status is `offline` or `error` and triggers a login for each. Login happens in the background; per-bot status updates arrive via the `bot:statusChanged` socket event.

count.description: Number of bots a connect was triggered for

post.summary: Disconnect every bot that is not offline
post.description: Requires the `manage_bots` permission. Iterates all bots whose status is not `offline` and disconnects each. Per-bot status updates arrive via the `bot:statusChanged` socket event.

post.summary: Upload one image and apply it as the Steam profile avatar to every connected bot account
post.description: Requires the `manage_bots` permission. The image is normalized to a 512×512 JPEG before being relayed to each bot via the lobby bot service. Bots that are not currently online are skipped with an error in the result list.

avatar.description: JPEG, PNG, or WebP image, max 5 MB

put.description: Updates info and roles. MMR is intentionally NOT accepted here — it changes only via the MMR Verifications approval flow (POST /api/mmr-verifications + admin approve).

post.description: Awards 10 XP for days 1-6, 20 XP for day 7+. Streak resets if a day is missed.
Requires manage_competitions permission

post.summary: Manually trigger the Discord embed for this competition
post.description: Requires manage_competitions on this comp (or full manage_competitions perm). Calls the bot's POST /internal/tournament/announce. Stamps competitions.discord_announced_at on success — re-announces are allowed (admin can correct typos and post again, the previous timestamp is shown in the UI).

502.description: Bot rejected announcement (plugin disabled, channel not configured, etc.)

get.description: Anyone with manage rights on this comp (creator, helper, or global manage_competitions) can read the list.

200.description: { helpers: [{ player_id, name, avatar_url, steam_id, added_at, added_by }] }

post.description: OWNER or global manage_competitions only. Helpers themselves cannot add other helpers (prevents a helper from removing the owner). The added player gains the same scoped management rights as the owner on this comp (treated as `created_by` by requireCompPermission), but no global perms.

400.description: Invalid player_id, or target is already the owner

403.description: Caller is not the owner / global admin

post.description: Requires manage_captains permission

Available only when settings.teamRegistrationMode is true and settings.teamRegistrationOpen is true. The authenticated user becomes a captain of a new team and registers (settings.teamRegistrationSize - 1) team members searched by name or Steam ID. Auction phase is suppressed in this mode.

captainRole.description: Optional playing role for the captain (1-5)

400.description: Mode disabled, validation failed, or invalid body

403.description: Banned, registration closed, or outside registration window

409.description: Conflict: already a captain, team name taken, member already on a team, or member already a captain

post.summary: Sync playing roles from match stats
post.description: Sets each player's playing_role based on their most recent lane_role from OpenDota match stats. Safe lane players with low GPM are assigned pos 5 (support), others pos 1 (carry). Requires manage_players permission.

Self-registration must be enabled via settings.allowSteamRegistration (admins bypass this). For users with an admin-verified MMR (players.mmr_verified_at IS NOT NULL), the submitted mmr is ignored — competition_players.mmr mirrors the verified players.mmr.

mmr.description: Submitted MMR for this competition. Ignored when the caller's players.mmr_verified_at is set — both players.mmr and competition_players.mmr remain equal to the admin-verified value.

403.description: Self-registration disabled, banned, or outside registration window

post.summary: Assign player to a captain's team
post.description: Manually drafts a player to a captain's team. Requires manage_players permission.
Removes a player from their drafted team (sets drafted=0, clears drafted_by). Requires manage_players permission.

post.summary: Admin: add user to competition pool
Import a Steam user and add as competition participant
post.description: Finds or creates a global player by Steam ID, then adds them to the competition participant pool. Used for bulk importing participants from Steam ID lists.

post.summary: Admin: remove user from competition pool

200.description: Auction state with captains, players, bids, settings
Array of captains with their drafted players

get.summary: Get upcoming scheduled matches across all active competitions

200.description: Array of upcoming matches with team and competition info, ordered by scheduled_at ascending (max 20)
Tournament state with matches and team rosters. Response includes `rosters` object keyed by captain ID, each containing array of {player_id, name, avatar, is_captain}

post.summary: Custom bracket: create a match inside a draft custom_bracket stage
Custom bracket: validate and flip a draft stage to active

400.description: Validation failed — `errors` array contains per-slot issues

patch.summary: Custom bracket: update best_of / round / match_order / label (pending matches only)
Custom bracket: hard-seed a match slot (rejects if slot has an incoming link)
Custom bracket: update winner/loser forward links; cycle-checked

delete.summary: Custom bracket: delete a match from a draft stage (blocked if referenced)

post.summary: Create a template from an existing competition's settings

put.summary: Update match team penalty settings (overrides competition defaults for this match)

200.description: Updated. Also auto-fetches OpenDota stats for games with dotabuff_id.

get.summary: Get player stats for a match game
get.description: Returns OpenDota player stats (kills, deaths, assists, wards, multi-kills, etc.) and picks_bans array for a specific game in a match.

200.description: Player stats array and picks_bans array with {hero_id, is_pick, team, order}

post.summary: Refetch OpenDota stats for a match game
post.description: Re-fetches player stats from OpenDota API for the given game. Requires admin permission. Useful if the match replay was not yet parsed on first fetch.

400.description: No match ID set for this game

get.summary: Preview placement XP awards per stage
get.description: Returns each non-group stage with its would-be 1st/2nd/3rd placements: captain + team, player list with alreadyAwarded flag, XP amount, and a `blocked` reason if the award can't run (e.g. pending matches, missing final winner). Requires competition admin permission.

blocked.description: Reason awards cannot run, or null if ready

post.summary: Award placement XP for a stage
post.description: Awards 1st/2nd/3rd placement XP to all players on placed teams and marks the stage completed. Idempotent via XP log unique constraint — re-running skips already-awarded rows. Returns counts of awarded vs skipped entries. Requires competition admin permission.

409.description: Stage not eligible — see error message (pending matches, no final winner, group stage, etc.)

get.summary: Get fantasy stages and user picks
get.description: Returns all fantasy stages (with allowed_captain_ids and match assignments), the current user's picks, lockedPicks (picks from disallowed teams that can't be changed), and repeat info.
Returns fantasy points leaderboard for active/completed stages.

200.description: Leaderboard with users and points per stage

allowedCaptainIds.description: Captain IDs whose players can be picked. Null or omitted = all teams allowed.
Captain IDs whose players can be picked. Null = all teams allowed. Existing picks from removed teams become locked.

put.summary: Save fantasy picks for a stage
put.description: Save user's player picks for each role. Stage must be in 'pending' status. All 5 roles required, no duplicate players. If fantasyEnforceRoles is enabled, players can only be picked for roles matching their playing_role.

get.summary: Get detailed per-game point breakdown for a player
get.description: Returns a detailed per-game, per-stat point breakdown for a specific player evaluated in a given fantasy role within a stage. Used by the 'Check Player' tab.

parameters.name: role
parameters.description: Fantasy role to evaluate points with

get.description: Returns all users' picks across all stages for a competition. Requires competition admin permission.

200.description: All picks, stages, users, competition players, and captains

put.summary: Set a fantasy pick for a user (admin)
put.description: Admin sets or updates a fantasy pick for a specific user, stage, and role. No stage status restriction.

delete.summary: Clear a fantasy pick for a user (admin)
delete.description: Admin clears a specific fantasy pick for a user. No stage status restriction.

post.description: Requires manage_news permission
Vote 1 for like, -1 for dislike, 0 to remove vote. Toggles if same vote is sent again.

get.description: Requires authentication. Returns all leagues with public=true (visible to everyone who can create tournaments) plus the caller's own private leagues. manage_leagues / admins see every row.

post.description: Requires manage_leagues or manage_own_leagues permission. Set public=true to make the league usable by everyone in the lobby league dropdown; otherwise it is only visible to the creator and manage_leagues holders. dota_league_id is unique across the table — registering an id another row already holds returns 409.

409.description: dota_league_id already registered by another row

put.description: Requires manage_leagues, or manage_own_leagues if the caller created the league. Changing dota_league_id to one another row already holds returns 409.

409.description: dota_league_id already registered by another row

delete.description: Requires manage_leagues, or manage_own_leagues if the caller created the league.

get.description: Returns only is_active=true plans, no subscriber counts. Intended for the future public pricing page.
Requires manage_subscription_plans. Includes inactive plans and active_subscriber_count for each.

post.description: Requires manage_subscription_plans. Slug is auto-derived from name if omitted; collisions return 409.

put.description: Requires manage_subscription_plans. All fields are optional — only the provided fields are updated.

delete.description: Requires manage_subscription_plans. Returns 409 if any user_subscriptions row references the plan — the FK is ON DELETE RESTRICT to keep payment history intact. Mark inactive instead.

409.description: Plan has subscriber history; mark inactive instead

post.description: Requires manage_subscription_plans. Multipart/form-data with field `badge`. PNG / JPG / WebP up to 5 MB. Server resizes to 64×64 PNG (cover/center) and replaces any existing badge file.

delete.description: Requires manage_subscription_plans. Unlinks the file and clears badge_url.

get.summary: List subscribers (active + cancelled history) for a plan
get.description: Requires manage_subscription_plans. Active rows first, then cancelled history newest-first.

post.summary: Manually assign a player to a plan
post.description: Requires manage_subscription_plans. Cancels any existing active subscription on a different plan first (one active row per player). source = 'manual'.

delete.description: Requires manage_subscription_plans. Sets status='cancelled' and cancelled_at=NOW() — does not delete the row.

400.description: Subscription does not belong to this plan

get.description: Requires manage_users permission
Returns players with XP > 0 ranked by total_xp descending. Supports pagination.
Returns player info, competition history, tournament placements, top heroes, and lifetime stats aggregated from match_game_player_stats.

200.description: Player profile with competition history, tournament results, and stats block

stats.description: Lifetime aggregates from match_game_player_stats. Null if player has no steam_id.

kda_sample_size.description: Actual number of games used (up to 10)

get.description: Returns paginated per-game rows (one row per parsed match_game). Each row includes the player's hero, KDA, duration, win/loss, and per-side kill totals (radiant_kills / dire_kills) used as the in-game score. Empty list when the player has no steam_id.

200.description: Paginated game rows with total count
Array of XP log entries (most recent 50)
Paginated XP log with total count

get.description: Returns team info, roster, match history, and win/loss stats for a captain's team.

200.description: Team profile with roster and matches

post.summary: Ban a player and capture admin identity + reason
post.description: Sets is_banned=true and records banned_at, banned_by (calling admin), and banned_reason. Requires `manage_users` permission.

reason.description: Optional explanation visible to other admins on unban.

post.summary: Unban a player and clear ban metadata
post.description: Sets is_banned=false and nulls banned_at, banned_by, banned_reason. Requires `manage_users` permission.

get.summary: List my accepted friends (with online flag)
List my pending friend requests (incoming + outgoing)

post.description: Creates a `pending` row. If the addressee already has a pending request out to me, both rows collapse into a single `accepted` friendship (auto-accept). Rejects if either side blocked the other (403), if a duplicate exists (409), or if invalid (400).

403.description: Either side has blocked the other

post.description: Only the addressee of the pending row may accept. Sets status=accepted and responded_at=NOW().

delete.summary: Cancel outgoing, reject incoming, or unfriend
delete.description: Either party can delete an accepted friendship. For pending rows only the requester (cancel) or addressee (reject) can call. Does not unblock — use /api/friends/blocks/{id}.

403.description: Not your friendship, or row is a block

post.description: Drops any existing pending/accepted rows between the pair (in either direction) and inserts a directional `blocked` row. Both directions can have independent blocks.

delete.description: Only the blocker can call. Deletes the directional block row.

post.description: Applies a signed delta to the player's dotacoins balance and writes a row to dotacoin_transactions for audit. Resulting balance must not go negative. Requires `manage_users` permission.

delta.description: Signed amount to add (positive) or subtract (negative). Must be non-zero.

reason.description: Optional note recorded with the transaction (≤280 chars).

400.description: Invalid delta or resulting balance would be negative

get.description: Returns games with dotabuff IDs that haven't been fully parsed by OpenDota yet. Requires manage_games permission.

get.summary: Get all games with match IDs
get.description: Returns all games that have dotabuff IDs, regardless of parse status. Used for force refetch all. Requires manage_games permission.

post.description: Re-fetches OpenDota stats including picks/bans for a specific match game. Requires manage_games permission.

200.description: Refetch result with saved count and parsed status

get.description: Returns the public site-settings payload. `sockets_enabled` is the realtime kill switch — when `false`, the server refuses new Socket.io handshakes and the in-flight ones have been disconnected.

site_topbar_enabled.description: When true, every page renders a top-bar notification with site_topbar_html. Dismissible per-session, keyed by HTML hash so updates re-show.

site_topbar_html.description: Sanitized HTML (Tiptap → v-safe-html allowlist). Supports text formatting, links, images.

put.description: Requires manage_site_settings permission. Setting `sockets_enabled: false` immediately disconnects all currently-connected Socket.io clients and refuses new handshakes until re-enabled. The flag is persisted, so it survives server restarts.

get.description: Requires manage_discord_settings. Returns persisted discord_* keys (guild ID, role IDs, welcome channel, auto-verify toggle). Empty string for unset keys.

put.description: Requires manage_discord_settings. Triggers an async POST /internal/reload-settings on the bot so changes apply within ~1s instead of waiting for the 60s poll.

get.summary: List Discord roles via the bot
get.description: Requires manage_discord_settings. Proxies the bot's GET /internal/roles. Returns roles for the configured guild.

200.description: { guildId, roles: [{ id, name, color, position, managed, hoist, mentionable }] }

get.summary: List Discord channels via the bot
get.description: Requires manage_discord_settings. Proxies the bot's GET /internal/channels.

200.description: { guildId, channels: [{ id, name, type, parentId, position }] }

get.summary: List Discord guild members for the AdminUsersPage link picker
get.description: Requires manage_users. Proxies the bot's GET /internal/members. Bots are filtered out, list is sorted by displayName.

200.description: { guildId, members: [{ id, username, displayName, avatarUrl }] }

put.summary: Manually link a Discord account to a player
put.description: Requires manage_users. Sets players.discord_id + discord_username. Refuses with 409 if another player already holds that discord_id (admin must unlink the conflicting side first).

409.description: Discord account is already linked to a different player

delete.summary: Unlink the Discord account from a player
delete.description: Requires manage_users. Clears players.discord_id and discord_username.

post.description:
Bot-only endpoint (bearer auth). Re-emits the payload as `client.emit(type, payload)` so any plugin with `@EventHook async on<Type>(payload)` receives it. Plugin gating (discord_plugin_<name>_enabled) applies automatically. Use this for new server-side events that don't need bot-internal side effects — for events that also create voice channels / etc, add a dedicated endpoint that does the inline work AND emits.

Known event types are catalogued in discord/src/core/draft-events.ts.

payload.description: Event-specific shape; see DRAFT_EVENTS interfaces in the bot.

post.summary: Bot-internal: create per-team voice channels and pull players from In-House
post.description: Bot-only endpoint (bearer auth on the bot's :3030 internal HTTP server) called by the draft server when a queue match's lobby is created. Creates two voice channels (Radiant + Dire), restricts them to the team's discord-linked players + the Caster role, and moves any of those players currently sitting in the configured In-House voice channel into their team channel. Players not in In-House are skipped. Idempotent per matchId — returns reason on duplicate.

200.description: { ok, radiantChannelId, direChannelId, movedCount } or { ok: false, reason }

post.summary: Bot-internal: schedule cleanup of per-team voice channels
post.description: Bot-only endpoint. Called by the draft server when a queue match completes (via OpenDota auto-fill) or is cancelled. After the configured delay (default 10 min, or immediate=true on cancel), the bot moves any stragglers back to In-House and deletes the team channels.

immediate.description: Skip the cleanup delay (used for cancellations).

get.summary: List bot plugins (autoVerify, welcome, …) with current enabled state
get.description:
Requires manage_discord_settings. Proxies the bot's GET /internal/plugins. Each plugin's enabled state reflects the persisted `discord_plugin_<name>_enabled` setting (default = the plugin's @Plugin({enabled}) info value, true if absent).

To toggle: PUT /api/admin/discord/settings with `{ discord_plugin_<name>_enabled: 'true' | 'false' }`. The bot is reloaded immediately; gating is enforced centrally in PluginManager.bindHooks so no plugin code change is required.
Requires manage_discord_settings. Hits the bot's /internal/health (no auth) and reports reachable/ready/settingsLoaded.

200.description: { reachable, ready, bot, settingsLoaded } or { reachable: false, error }

get.summary: Get live Dota 2 streamers from registered users
Per-player W/L over the last N completed queue matches in a pool
get.description: Returns win/loss counts computed from each player's most recent completed queue matches in the given pool. Used by the in-queue roster to show a mini form guide.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: How many recent completed matches per player to aggregate over

200.description: Array of per-player W/L; players with no completed matches in the pool are omitted

get.description: Returns all queue pools including disabled ones. Requires manage_queue_pools (full) or manage_own_queue_pools (limited to your own pools).

post.description: Creates a new queue pool. Requires manage_queue_pools (full) or manage_own_queue_pools (limited to your own pools).

put.description: Updates an existing queue pool. Requires manage_queue_pools (full) or manage_own_queue_pools (limited to your own pools). Only provided fields are updated.

delete.description: Deletes a queue pool and cascades to queue_matches. Requires manage_queue_pools (full) or manage_own_queue_pools (limited to your own pools).

get.summary: Public: latest live snapshot for a queue match (back-compat)
get.description: Translates queue_match.id → matches.id internally and returns the same snapshot as /api/matches/{matchId}/live. Returns 200 + null when no poll is active.

get.summary: Public: latest live snapshot for any match (queue or tournament)
get.description: Returns the in-memory snapshot from liveMatchPoller — team scores, game time, game state, and per-player heroes/level/KDA/NW/items. Returns 200 + null when the poller is not running (match not live, or STEAM_API_KEY missing). Used by both QueueMatchPage and MatchRoomPage on mount so a refresh / late-join sees current state without waiting up to 12s for the next poll tick.

post.summary: Admin: manually inject server_steam_id for a stuck match
post.description: When the bot didn't surface a server_steam_id and the player-summary bootstrap failed (private Steam Game Details), an admin can paste the value here. Persists onto the latest match_lobbies row and pushes it into the running poller (or starts polling fresh). Permissions: admin, manage_competitions / manage_own_competitions for tournament matches, manage_queue_pools / manage_own_queue_pools for queue matches.

server_steam_id.description: The lobby's server_steam_id from server logs

200.description: Updated. Response.injected=true if an existing poller picked it up, false if a fresh poll was started.

post.summary: Admin: stop and restart the live poller for a match
post.description: Useful when the bootstrap loop has given up after 30 attempts. Same permissions as /live-server-id.

get.description: Returns full match info including team rosters, captains, pool, games (with has_stats flag), and — when the match is attached to a season — a `season` object plus per-player season fields: `season_delta`, `points_before`, `points_after` (from season_match_log for this match), and `season_points` / `season_games` / `season_wins` / `season_losses` (current standings).

200.description: Queue match with rosters, games, and optional season block

get.description: Returns all queue matches in picking, lobby_creating, or live status. Enriched with in-memory state and lobby info. Requires manage_queue_pools (full) or manage_own_queue_pools (limited to your own pools).

200.description: Array of active queue matches with team rosters and lobby info

post.description: Cancels an active queue match. Notifies all players via socket, cleans up in-memory state, and sets DB status to cancelled. Refuses once the latest match_lobbies row is 'completed' (bot has left the lobby after game_started) — at that point the Dota match is running on its own. Requires manage_queue_pools (full) or manage_own_queue_pools (limited to your own pools).

409.description: Match already started — bot has left the lobby

post.summary: Admin: force-complete a stuck queue match
post.description: For matches whose Dota game ended but never got cleaned up by _autoFillGameWinner (e.g. server restart mid-game, bot lost connection, OpenDota never returned a winner). Marks queue_matches.status = 'completed', frees every roster player from the in-memory playerInMatch lock so they can re-queue, broadcasts queue:cancelled to the queue-match room. Skips bot lobby cancel since this is meant for matches the bot has already left. Requires manage_queue_pools or manage_own_queue_pools (scoped to owned pools).

get.summary: Admin: list currently queued players across all pools
get.description: Returns the in-memory queue roster for every pool, each entry decorated with the poolId it's queued in. Requires manage_queue_pools (full) or manage_own_queue_pools (limited to your own pools).

post.summary: Admin: kick a player out of their current queue
Admin: set a player's shadow-pool flag
post.description: Updates players.shadow_pool. 0 = none (matches normally), 1 = soft shadow (marked but matches normally), 2 = hard shadow (only included in a match-of-10 when at least one other shadow-flagged player is in the same group). The flag is a global player attribute; requires manage_queue_pools (manage_own_queue_pools is rejected). If the player is currently queued, the in-memory entry is updated and a queue:updated broadcast pushes the badge change to all clients in that pool.

get.description: Each row carries pool_id (null = ban applies to every pool) and pool_name. Optional ?pool_id=N to filter to one pool, or ?pool_id=global to filter to global bans. manage_own_queue_pools holders only see bans on pools they created (global bans are hidden).

parameters.name: pool_id
parameters.description: Pool id, or 'global' for NULL pool_id

post.summary: Admin: create or update a queue ban
post.description: If pool_id is null/omitted, the ban applies to every pool ('global'). Otherwise it only blocks the player from queuing into that specific pool. manage_own_queue_pools holders must specify a pool they created — global bans require full manage_queue_pools.

pool_id.description: null = ban applies to all pools

delete.summary: Admin: remove a queue ban (by ban id)
delete.description: Removes a queue ban. manage_own_queue_pools holders can only remove bans on pools they created — global bans require full manage_queue_pools.

get.description: Returns active seasons plus seasons that ended within the last 30 days, with player and match counts. Public — no auth required.

Filters out players below the season's `min_games_for_leaderboard` setting. Sorted by points DESC, then games DESC, then name ASC. Each row includes the player's `shadow_pool` flag (0 = none, 1 = soft, 2 = hard) so the UI can colour the avatar ring.

post.description: Sends a screenshot file plus the requested MMR. Creates a pending submission. Replaces any existing pending submission by the same user (old screenshot file is unlinked).

200.description: Array of submissions with reviewer info

post.description: Marks the submission status as cancelled. The row and its screenshot are preserved so the admin still sees the attempt in the player's history.

get.description: Filter by status (pending, approved, rejected, cancelled, all) and optionally by playerId. Pending bubbles to the top. Requires manage_mmr_verifications.

200.description: Array of submissions enriched with player and reviewer info

post.summary: Admin: approve a submission and apply MMR
post.description: Sets players.mmr to submitted_mmr and marks the row approved. Requires manage_mmr_verifications.
Marks the row rejected with an optional note. Does not modify players.mmr.

get.summary: All seasons a player has played, with their rank within each
get.description: Returns one row per (player, season) where the player has a season_rankings entry. Includes the player's rank within the season computed via window function. Active seasons first, newest first.

get.summary: One player's stats and recent rating changes in a season

get.description: Includes pool/player/match counts. Requires manage_seasons.

post.description: Slug auto-generated from name if omitted. Settings JSON accepts: starting_points (default 1000), k_win (30), k_loss (15), mmr_scale (1500), min_points (0), max_points (null), min_games_for_leaderboard (5), strength_basis ('mmr' default — team strength = avg Dota MMR; or 'points' — team strength = avg season points). Requires manage_seasons.

verified_mmr_only.description: When true, only players with mmr_verified_at set can queue into pools assigned to this season.

get.summary: Admin: get one season + assigned pools

patch.description: Only provided fields are updated. settings is shallow-merged with existing settings JSON.

delete.description: Cascades season_rankings and season_match_log. Pools assigned to this season have their season_id set to NULL.

post.description: Writes a synthetic season_match_log row with queue_match_id = NULL so the audit trail stays complete. Also broadcasts season:rankUpdated.

delta.description: Positive or negative non-zero point change

post.summary: Admin: rebuild rankings by replaying every match + manual adjust
post.description: Wipes season_rankings and matches' audit-log rows, then replays every completed queue_match in this season under the current settings. Manual-adjust rows are preserved by timestamp and re-applied in order. Use after editing settings or to clean up corrupted state.

post.summary: Admin: claim past completed matches into this season, then recompute
post.description: Sets queue_matches.season_id on previously-orphaned (NULL) completed matches that belong to a pool currently assigned to this season and fall within the season's starts_at..ends_at window (either bound is optional). After claiming, runs a full recompute so the leaderboard reflects the new history. Idempotent — running again with no new orphans is a no-op.

get.summary: Headline counters for the homepage hero (active players, live matches, active tournaments)
The competition flagged is_featured=TRUE plus its current bracket slice
get.description: Returns null when no competition is featured. Bracket array contains up to 7 matches sorted by status then schedule.

200.description: Featured competition with captain_count and bracket[]

get.summary: Top players by MMR for the homepage leaderboard, with current streak from the most active season

200.description: { season, players[] }; each player carries streak {count, won} and win_rate from season_match_log

get.summary: Top heroes by pick count over the last N days

post.description: Multipart fields: logo (image), alt (string), link (URL). Appends to site_settings.site_sponsors JSON array.

put.summary: Admin: update a sponsor's alt text or link

delete.summary: Admin: delete a sponsor and unlink its logo file

get.summary: Get Dota 2 hero and item constants
get.description: Returns hero ID-to-name mappings and item ID-to-name mappings fetched from OpenDota API. Cached server-side for 24 hours. Response is also cacheable client-side for 1 hour.

heroes.description: Map of hero_id to hero data

items.description: Map of item_id to item data

post.summary: Manually retry lobby creation for a queue match
post.description: Admin-only. Marks any in-flight match_lobbies row for this match as errored, frees its bot, and dispatches a fresh create_lobby on a different available bot. Bypasses the automatic 3-attempt cap. Refuses once the latest match_lobbies row is 'completed' (bot has left the lobby after game_started).

409.description: Queue match is cancelled/completed, or bot has already left the lobby

500.description: No alternate bot available, or dispatch failed

post.description: Scans all completed matches in the competition and applies advancement for any downstream slot that's still empty. Idempotent — safe to call multiple times.

get.description: Requires `manage_jobs` permission. Returns rows, counts by status, and registered handler types.

post.summary: Requeue a failed or pending job
Bulk delete completed jobs older than N days

get.summary: Aggregate request/socket counts for the period
get.description: Returns total requests, unique users/IPs, avg + p95 latency, status-class breakdown, socket totals, and a `logging_enabled` flag reflecting the server's `REQUEST_LOG_DISABLED` env var (false → no new rows are being recorded). Optional userId/ip filters scope to a single entity. Requires `view_request_stats` permission.

200.description: Array of { method, path, count, avg_ms, p95_ms, errors }

get.description: Default bucket auto-selected from period (1h→minute, 24h→hour, 7d→6h, 14d→12h, longer→day). Optional path/method/userId/ip filters narrow the data.

200.description: { bucket, rows: [{ bucket, count, errors, avg_ms }] }

get.summary: Top authenticated users by request count

parameters.name: ip
parameters.description: When provided, only counts requests from this IP.

200.description: Array of { user_id, name, avatar_url, count, errors, last_seen }

get.summary: Top source IPs by request count

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: When provided, only counts requests by this user.

200.description: Array of { ip, count, unique_users, errors, last_seen }

get.summary: Top Socket.io events by emit count
Multi-account / alt detector for a single user
get.description: Cross-references the target user's IPs in the request_logs table with other accounts that hit the same IPs in the window. Used to detect alts. Requires `view_request_stats` permission.

get.summary: Top SPA page paths by visit count
get.description: Aggregates the `path` field of `socket_event_logs` rows where `event = 'activity'`. Captures Vue Router navigation events that never hit Express.
Searches by player id, steam id, fuzzy name; matches by id (queue or tournament) or dotabuff_id. Returns up to `limit` results per category.
Returns the visible nav items in render order. No auth required — frontend reads this on every page load.

put.summary: Bulk reorder by sending an array of ids

get.summary: Raw page-visit log for one user
get.description: Returns the most recent SPA navigation events for a given user. Requires userId filter.

get.summary: Raw recent request log for one user or IP
get.description: Returns the most recent request_logs rows for the given userId or ip filter. At least one of userId or ip is required (returns 400 otherwise). Used by the admin dashboard to show drill-down detail when a filter is active.

200.description: Array of { ts, method, path, status, duration_ms, user_id, ip, user_agent, user_name }
